reasoning: The FRED series GASDESW shows strong recent downward momentum, dropping roughly 4.5 cents per week over the three weeks leading up to the 2025-10-26 cutoff (declining from 3.754 on 2025-09-29 down to 3.620 on 2025-10-20). Given this strong short-term negative trend, the probability of a price increase within just one week (by Nov 2) is low. For the 1-month horizon (Nov 25), the downward momentum may start to stabilize, but it still favors prices being somewhat lower than the late October baseline, keeping the probability of an increase below 0.5. For the 3-month horizon (Jan 24), historical seasonal patterns often show an increase in distillate prices due to winter heating demand (e.g., from late Oct 2024 to late Jan 2025, prices recovered from 3.573 to 3.659), pushing the probability slightly above 0.5. As required, uncertainty strictly increases with horizon.
